name: Stuffed Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 2 cups cubed bread crumbs (I use a mix of white and WW)
1 1/2 tsp salad herbs (mix of parsley, marjoram, etc. I think McCormick makes this mix)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove mushroom stems. Chop the stems and set aside.
2. Bring a large pot of water to boil. Add the mushrooms and cook for a couple minutes or until they start to shrink. Drain the mushrooms in a colander.
3. Place the drained mushrooms into a pan of cold water for about 30 seconds to stop further cooking, then drain again.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. In a large non-stick saute pan, heat the wine over medium heat. Add the chopped onions, mushroom stems, minced garlic, and celery. Cook until the vegetables are tender.
6. Add the finely shredded carrot and salad herbs. Stir to combine.
7. Add the bread crumbs and mix well. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Fill each mushroom cap with a heaping tablespoon of the mixture.
9. Place the stuffed mushrooms on a lightly sprayed baking sheet.
10. Bake in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the tops are lightly browned.
